title: Vitamin D and respiratory infection
text: A randomized controlled trial found that daily vitamin D supplementation reduced the incidence of acute respiratory tract infections, with the strongest protective effect observed in participants who were vitamin D deficient at baseline.

title: Vitamin D bone density
text: Vitamin D regulates calcium absorption in the intestine and is essential for maintaining bone mineral density; severe deficiency causes rickets in children and osteomalacia in adults.

title: Sunlight and vitamin D synthesis
text: Cutaneous synthesis of vitamin D3 occurs when ultraviolet B radiation from sunlight converts 7-dehydrocholesterol in the skin, accounting for the majority of vitamin D in most populations.

title: Statins and LDL cholesterol
text: Statin therapy lowers low-density lipoprotein cholesterol by inhibiting HMG-CoA reductase, the rate-limiting enzyme of hepatic cholesterol biosynthesis, and reduces the risk of major cardiovascular events.

title: Statin muscle side effects
text: Myalgia and elevated creatine kinase are recognized adverse effects of statin treatment; statin-associated muscle symptoms lead a minority of patients to discontinue therapy.

title: Dietary fiber and cholesterol
text: Soluble dietary fiber binds bile acids in the gut and modestly lowers serum LDL cholesterol, contributing to cardiovascular risk reduction independent of pharmacologic treatment.

title: Aspirin and platelet aggregation
text: Low-dose aspirin irreversibly acetylates cyclooxygenase-1 in platelets, suppressing thromboxane A2 production and inhibiting platelet aggregation for the lifespan of the platelet.

title: Aspirin bleeding risk
text: The antiplatelet action of aspirin increases the risk of gastrointestinal and intracranial bleeding, which must be weighed against its cardiovascular benefit in primary prevention.

title: Insulin and blood glucose
text: Insulin secreted by pancreatic beta cells promotes glucose uptake in muscle and adipose tissue and suppresses hepatic gluconeogenesis, thereby lowering blood glucose concentration.

title: Type 2 diabetes insulin resistance
text: Type 2 diabetes is characterized by peripheral insulin resistance combined with progressive beta-cell dysfunction, leading to chronic hyperglycemia and microvascular complications.

title: Metformin mechanism
text: Metformin reduces hepatic glucose production primarily by activating AMP-activated protein kinase and is the first-line oral agent for type 2 diabetes management.

title: Exercise and insulin sensitivity
text: Regular aerobic exercise increases skeletal muscle glucose transporter expression and improves whole-body insulin sensitivity, lowering fasting glucose in insulin-resistant individuals.

title: Gut microbiome and obesity
text: The composition of the gut microbiota differs between lean and obese individuals, and transfer of microbiota from obese donors can increase adiposity in germ-free mice.

title: Antibiotics and microbiome disruption
text: Broad-spectrum antibiotic exposure depletes commensal gut bacteria and reduces microbial diversity, an effect that can persist for months after treatment ends.

title: Probiotics and digestive health
text: Certain probiotic strains reduce the duration of antibiotic-associated diarrhea by restoring beneficial gut bacteria, though effects are strain-specific and modest.

title: Telomere length and aging
text: Telomeres shorten with each cell division, and critically short telomeres trigger cellular senescence; shorter leukocyte telomere length is associated with increased mortality risk.

title: Telomerase in cancer cells
text: Most cancer cells reactivate telomerase to maintain telomere length, enabling unlimited proliferation; telomerase inhibition is being explored as an anticancer strategy.

title: Oxidative stress and cellular damage
text: Reactive oxygen species generated by mitochondrial metabolism damage DNA, proteins, and lipids; accumulated oxidative damage contributes to the cellular aging process.

title: Tumor suppressor p53
text: The tumor suppressor protein p53 halts the cell cycle and induces apoptosis in response to DNA damage; loss-of-function mutations in TP53 are found in roughly half of human cancers.

title: Angiogenesis in tumor growth
text: Solid tumors stimulate the growth of new blood vessels through vascular endothelial growth factor signaling, and anti-angiogenic drugs aim to starve tumors of their blood supply.

title: Immune checkpoint inhibitors
text: Antibodies blocking the PD-1 and CTLA-4 immune checkpoints release T cells from inhibition and produce durable responses in a subset of patients with metastatic melanoma.

title: Vaccine adaptive immunity
text: Vaccination primes the adaptive immune system to generate memory B and T cells, enabling a rapid and specific antibody response upon subsequent exposure to the pathogen.

title: Antibody neutralization of virus
text: Neutralizing antibodies bind viral surface proteins and block entry into host cells; high neutralizing titers correlate with protection against reinfection.

title: Herd immunity threshold
text: When a sufficient fraction of a population is immune, sustained transmission of a contagious pathogen cannot occur; the herd immunity threshold rises with the basic reproduction number.

title: Sleep and memory consolidation
text: Slow-wave sleep supports the consolidation of declarative memories by reactivating hippocampal traces and transferring them to neocortical storage during the night.

title: Sleep deprivation and cognition
text: Acute sleep deprivation impairs attention, working memory, and reaction time, and chronic short sleep is associated with elevated risk of metabolic and cardiovascular disease.

title: Caffeine and adenosine receptors
text: Caffeine promotes wakefulness by antagonizing adenosine receptors in the central nervous system, blocking the sleep-pressure signal that adenosine normally produces.

title: Circadian rhythm and melatonin
text: The suprachiasmatic nucleus coordinates circadian rhythms, and evening melatonin secretion from the pineal gland signals darkness to entrain the sleep-wake cycle.

title: Blood pressure and sodium intake
text: Higher dietary sodium intake raises blood pressure in salt-sensitive individuals, and population-level sodium reduction lowers the incidence of hypertension and stroke.

title: Exercise and cardiovascular fitness
text: Regular endurance exercise lowers resting heart rate and blood pressure, improves endothelial function, and reduces the long-term risk of coronary heart disease.

title: Smoking and lung cancer
text: Cigarette smoke contains carcinogens that induce mutations in bronchial epithelial cells; smoking is the leading cause of lung cancer and accounts for the majority of cases.

title: Air pollution and respiratory disease
text: Long-term exposure to fine particulate matter is associated with reduced lung function and increased rates of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease and respiratory mortality.

title: Omega-3 fatty acids and inflammation
text: Omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids from fish oil are precursors of anti-inflammatory mediators and may reduce circulating markers of systemic inflammation.

title: Mediterranean diet cardiovascular outcomes
text: Adherence to a Mediterranean diet rich in olive oil, nuts, and vegetables is associated with a reduced risk of major cardiovascular events in randomized dietary trials.

title: Obesity and metabolic syndrome
text: Central obesity is a core component of the metabolic syndrome, a clustering of insulin resistance, dyslipidemia, and hypertension that elevates cardiovascular and diabetes risk.

title: Hippocampus and spatial navigation
text: Place cells in the hippocampus fire at specific locations in an environment, forming a cognitive map that the brain uses for spatial navigation and memory.

title: Neuroplasticity and learning
text: Long-term potentiation strengthens synaptic connections in response to repeated activity, providing a cellular mechanism for learning and the formation of new memories.

title: Dopamine and reward learning
text: Midbrain dopamine neurons encode reward prediction errors, signaling the difference between expected and received reward to drive reinforcement learning in the brain.

title: Stress hormone cortisol
text: Cortisol released by the adrenal cortex during stress mobilizes glucose and suppresses non-essential functions; chronic elevation impairs hippocampal function and immune response.

title: Photosynthesis carbon fixation
text: During photosynthesis, the Calvin cycle fixes atmospheric carbon dioxide into organic sugars using ATP and NADPH generated by the light-dependent reactions in chloroplasts.
